mechanism_narrative: Strong and sustained retail sales growth indicates robust consumer demand, which directly translates to higher corporate revenues and earnings, thereby driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending is a major component of GDP
failure_condition: If RSAFS shows consistent positive month-over-month growth, but S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in more than 60% of such instances over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant drop in the unemployment rate below a critical threshold signals an overheating labor market, which can lead to wage inflation and prompt the central bank to tighten monetary policy, negatively impacting equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:Low unemployment can signal inflationary pressures
failure_condition: If UNRATE falls below 4.0% and S&P 500 20-day returns are predominantly positive for 6 consecutive months after the trigger.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth) is high and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is also rising, it indicates an aggressive monetary tightening cycle to combat inflation, which typically leads to higher discount rates and negative equity performance over a longer horizon.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth > 3.0% AND FEDFUNDS rises by > 0.25% in 3 months
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS meet the trigger conditions, but S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of such instances over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic expansion, strong housing starts (HOUST) signal robust consumer and business confidence, driving demand for goods and services, which positively impacts corporate earnings and S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing is a leading economic indicator
failure_condition: If HOUST exceeds 1000k units during an economic expansion, but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 40% of such periods over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates a healthy and expanding manufacturing sector, reflecting overall economic strength, which leads to higher corporate profits and positive S&P 500 returns over a longer horizon.
failure_condition: If INDPRO shows consistent growth (e.g., > 0.2% month-over-month for 6 consecutive months) but S&P 500 60-day returns are persistently negative over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals strong job creation, boosting consumer confidence and spending, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases by > 200k in a month
failure_condition: If PAYEMS increases by more than 200k in a month, but S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of such instances over a 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth (GDPC1) is strong but retail sales (RSAFS) are declining, it suggests a divergence where overall economic output is robust but consumer spending is weakening, potentially signaling a future slowdown and negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth > 3% AND RSAFS declines month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:Divergence in economic indicators can signal turning points
failure_condition: If GDPC1 growth is strong while RSAFS declines, but S&P 500 20-day returns are consistently positive over a 3-year period following such triggers.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-inflation environment, a stable or declining federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) provides accommodative monetary conditions, reducing borrowing costs and supporting equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a short horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Low rates and low inflation are generally supportive of equities
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL meet the trigger conditions, but S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in more than 60% of such instances over any 2-year rolling window.
